---
title: "CCHS Analytical Dataset: Univariate Distributions"
subtitle: "Data Primer 1 — Complete Variable Reference by Section"
author: "Analysis Team"
date: today
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    theme: yeti
    highlight-style: breeze
    code-line-numbers: false
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true
    keep-md: false
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Mission

This document provides a univariate distribution profile for every variable in the `cchs_analytical` table produced by the Ellis lane (`manipulation/2-ellis.R`). Variables are grouped according to the seven sections defined in `analysis/data-primer-1/variable-inclusion.md`, which traces each variable back to the §2.2 requirements in `stats_instructions_v3.md`.

For each numeric/continuous variable, a **summary statistics** table is shown (N valid, N missing, mean, SD, min, p25, median, p75, max). For each discrete/categorical variable, a **frequency table** is shown (value, n, % of non-missing). Variables that are all-NA in the current PUMF files are flagged accordingly.

**N = 63 843 rows** (CCHS 2010–2011 and CCHS 2013–2014 pooled, employment-eligible respondents).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
#| label: knitr-setup
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| warning: false
cat("Working directory: ", getwd())
library(knitr)
opts_knit$set(root.dir = "../../")
```

```{r}
#| label: set-options
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
#| message: false
cat("Working directory: ", getwd())
report_render_start_time <- Sys.time()
opts_chunk$set(
  results  = "show",
  message  = FALSE,
  warning  = FALSE,
  comment  = NA,
  tidy     = FALSE
)
echo_chunks    <- FALSE
message_chunks <- FALSE
options(width = 110)
read_chunk("analysis/data-primer-1/univariate-distributions.R")
```

# Environment

```{r load-packages}
```

```{r load-sources}
```

```{r declare-globals}
```

```{r declare-functions}
```

# Data

```{r load-data}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Outcome Variables

The **primary outcome** is `days_absent_total` — the total number of self-reported workdays lost in the past three months due to any health reason. The **sensitivity outcome** `days_absent_chronic` counts only days lost attributed to the respondent's own chronic condition. Both are derived by Ellis from eight LOP-module components (`lopg040`–`lopg100`); the components are also retained in the table.

## 1.1 Primary and Sensitivity Outcomes

```{r section-outcomes}
```

```{r display-outcomes}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_outcomes_summary,
  col.names = c("Variable", "N valid", "N missing", "Mean", "SD",
                "Min", "P25", "Median", "P75", "Max"),
  caption   = "Table 1.1 — Summary statistics: primary and sensitivity outcomes",
  align     = c("l", rep("r", 9))
)
```

## 1.2 LOP Component Variables

The eight raw LOP components contributing to `days_absent_total`. Each measures days absent for a specific health reason. High missingness is structural — the LOP module is only administered to currently employed respondents who reported any absence.

```{r display-lop}
#| echo: false
lop_display <- t_lop_summary[, c("label", "variable", "n_valid", "n_miss",
                                  "mean", "sd", "min", "p25", "median", "p75", "max")]
knitr::kable(
  lop_display,
  col.names = c("Reason", "Variable", "N valid", "N missing", "Mean", "SD",
                "Min", "P25", "Median", "P75", "Max"),
  caption   = "Table 1.2 — Summary statistics: LOP component variables",
  align     = c("l", "l", rep("r", 9))
)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 2. Chronic Conditions (Primary Exposure)

Seventeen of the 19 requested chronic conditions are available in the PUMF. Two are suppressed for confidentiality (`ccc_300` — other mental illness; `ccc_185` — digestive disease) and are absent from this dataset. All variables are binary factors (`"Yes"` / `"No"`); percentages are calculated among non-missing responses.

```{r section-chronic-conditions}
```

```{r display-chronic-conditions}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_chronic_conditions,
  col.names = c("Condition", "Yes (n)", "Yes (%)", "No (n)", "No (%)", "Missing (n)"),
  caption   = "Table 2.1 — Prevalence of 17 available chronic conditions (17 of 19 from §2.2 available in PUMF)",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r")
)
```

> **Note:** Two conditions from §2.2 are absent from the PUMF and therefore not shown: *other mental illness* (`ccc_300`) and *digestive disease* (`ccc_185`). Both require RDC access.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 3. Predisposing Variables

Predisposing variables characterise the respondent's socio-demographic background. Homeownership and student status are all-NA in the current output (source variables absent from PUMF). Children-by-age-group variables (`dhhgle5`, `dhhg611`) require a whitelist fix and are absent.

```{r section-predisposing}
```

::: panel-tabset
### Age Group

```{r display-age-group}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_pred_freq[["age_group"]],
  col.names = c("Age group", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "age_group — Recoded from dhhgage; Ordered: 15-24 / 25-54 / 55-75",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

### Sex

```{r display-sex}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_pred_freq[["sex"]],
  col.names = c("Sex", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "sex — Binary: Male / Female",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

### Marital Status

```{r display-marital-status}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_pred_freq[["marital_status"]],
  col.names = c("Marital status", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "marital_status — Single / Married / Common-law / Widowed-Divorced-Separated",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

### Education

```{r display-education}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_pred_freq[["education"]],
  col.names = c("Education level", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "education — Ordered factor: Less than secondary → Post-secondary graduate",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

### Immigration Status

```{r display-immigration-status}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_pred_freq[["immigration_status"]],
  col.names = c("Immigration status", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "immigration_status — Non-immigrant / Immigrant / Non-permanent resident",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

### Visible Minority

```{r display-visible-minority}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_pred_freq[["visible_minority"]],
  col.names = c("Ethnic origin", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "visible_minority — White / Visible minority",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

### Homeownership

```{r display-homeownership}
#| echo: false
n_miss_hw <- attr(t_pred_freq[["homeownership"]], "n_miss")
n_valid_hw <- attr(t_pred_freq[["homeownership"]], "n_valid")
if (n_valid_hw == 0) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "**All-NA variable.** `homeownership` is all-missing in the current output (%d missing, %d valid). ",
    n_miss_hw, n_valid_hw
  ))
  cat("A whitelist alias fix (`dhhdglvg` → `dhhglvg`) is required in `manipulation/2-ellis.R`.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    t_pred_freq[["homeownership"]],
    col.names = c("Homeownership", "N", "% (of valid)"),
    caption   = "homeownership — Living arrangement / homeownership status"
  )
}
```

### Student Status

```{r display-student-status}
#| echo: false
n_miss_ss <- attr(t_pred_freq[["student_status"]], "n_miss")
n_valid_ss <- attr(t_pred_freq[["student_status"]], "n_valid")
if (n_valid_ss == 0) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "**All-NA variable.** `student_status` is all-missing (%d missing, %d valid). ",
    n_miss_ss, n_valid_ss
  ))
  cat("`sdcdgstud` is suppressed in the CCHS PUMF; this variable requires RDC access.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    t_pred_freq[["student_status"]],
    col.names = c("Student status", "N", "% (of valid)"),
    caption   = "student_status"
  )
}
```

### Raw Age Code (dhhgage)

```{r display-dhhgage}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_dhhgage_summary,
  col.names = c("Variable", "N valid", "N missing", "Mean", "SD",
                "Min", "P25", "Median", "P75", "Max"),
  caption   = "dhhgage — Raw PUMF age group code (integer 1–16; not recoded to years in PUMF)",
  align     = c("l", rep("r", 9))
)
```
:::

> **Missing household-composition variables:** `dhhgle5` (children \< 5 yrs), `dhhg611` (children 6–11 yrs) — require whitelist name fixes and are absent from the current output. `dhhdfc12p` (children ≥ 12 yrs) has no PUMF equivalent. Household size (`dhhdghsz`) is present via alias resolution. See `variable-inclusion.qmd` §4 for details.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 4. Facilitating Variables

Facilitating variables describe health-system access, health behaviours, and labour-market context. Three variables are all-NA due to absent PUMF source variables: `alcohol_type` (`alcdgtyp`), `bmi_category` (`hwtdgbmi`), and `occupation_category` (`noc_31`). Income, employment type, and work schedule are populated via alias resolution.

```{r section-facilitating}
```

::: panel-tabset
### Smoking Status

```{r display-smoking}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_facil_freq[["smoking_status"]],
  col.names = c("Smoking status", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "smoking_status — Ordered: Never / Former / Occasional / Daily"
)
```

### Physical Activity

```{r display-physical-activity}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_facil_freq[["physical_activity"]],
  col.names = c("Physical activity level", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "physical_activity — Ordered: Active / Moderately active / Inactive"
)
```

### Job Stress

```{r display-job-stress}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_facil_freq[["job_stress"]],
  col.names = c("Job stress level", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "job_stress — Ordered: Not at all stressful → Extremely stressful"
)
```

### Has Family Doctor

```{r display-family-doctor}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_facil_freq[["has_family_doctor"]],
  col.names = c("Has regular family doctor", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "has_family_doctor — Yes / No"
)
```

### Income (5-cat)

```{r display-income}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_facil_freq[["income_5cat"]],
  col.names = c("Household income category", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "income_5cat \u2014 5-category derived household income",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

### Employment Type

```{r display-employment-type}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_facil_freq[["employment_type"]],
  col.names = c("Employment type", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "employment_type \u2014 Self-employed vs. Employee",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

### Work Schedule

```{r display-work-schedule}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_facil_freq[["work_schedule"]],
  col.names = c("Work schedule", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "work_schedule \u2014 Full-time vs. Part-time",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

### Alcohol Use

```{r display-alcohol}
#| echo: false
n_valid_al <- attr(t_facil_freq[["alcohol_type"]], "n_valid")
if (n_valid_al == 0) {
  cat("**All-NA variable.** `alcohol_type` is all-missing. ",
      "Whitelist alias fix (`alcdgtyp` → `alcdttm`) required in `manipulation/2-ellis.R`.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    t_facil_freq[["alcohol_type"]],
    col.names = c("Type of drinker", "N", "% (of valid)"),
    caption   = "alcohol_type — Derived type of drinker"
  )
}
```

### BMI Category

```{r display-bmi}
#| echo: false
n_valid_bmi <- attr(t_facil_freq[["bmi_category"]], "n_valid")
if (n_valid_bmi == 0) {
  cat("**All-NA variable.** `bmi_category` is all-missing. ",
      "Whitelist alias fix (`hwtdgbmi` → `hwtgbmi`) required in `manipulation/2-ellis.R`.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    t_facil_freq[["bmi_category"]],
    col.names = c("BMI category", "N", "% (of valid)"),
    caption   = "bmi_category — Derived BMI classification"
  )
}
```

### Occupation Category

```{r display-occupation}
#| echo: false
n_valid_oc <- attr(t_facil_freq[["occupation_category"]], "n_valid")
if (n_valid_oc == 0) {
  cat("**All-NA variable.** `occupation_category` is all-missing. ",
      "`noc_31` (NOC major group) is suppressed in the CCHS PUMF; requires RDC access.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    t_facil_freq[["occupation_category"]],
    col.names = c("Occupation category", "N", "% (of valid)"),
    caption   = "occupation_category — NOC major group"
  )
}
```

### Province of Residence

```{r display-province}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_province_freq,
  col.names = c("Province code (geodgprv)", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "geodgprv — Province / territory of residence (integer PUMF code; not recoded to names)"
)
```
:::

> **Missing facilitating variable:** `fvcdtot` (total daily fruit & vegetable consumption) requires a whitelist fix (`fvcdgtot` → `fvcdtot`) and is absent from the current output.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 5. Needs Variables

Needs variables capture self-reported health status and functional limitations, representing predisposing demand for healthcare and productivity loss.

```{r section-needs}
```

::: panel-tabset
### General Health

```{r display-self-health-general}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_needs_freq[["self_health_general"]],
  col.names = c("Self-perceived general health", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "self_health_general — Ordered: Excellent / Very good / Good / Fair / Poor"
)
```

### Mental Health

```{r display-self-health-mental}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_needs_freq[["self_health_mental"]],
  col.names = c("Self-perceived mental health", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "self_health_mental — Ordered: Excellent / Very good / Good / Fair / Poor"
)
```

### Health vs. Last Year

```{r display-health-vs-lastyear}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_needs_freq[["health_vs_lastyear"]],
  col.names = c("Health compared to 1 year ago", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "health_vs_lastyear — Ordered: Much better → Much worse"
)
```

### Activity Limitation

```{r display-activity-limitation}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_needs_freq[["activity_limitation"]],
  col.names = c("Activity / functional limitation", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "activity_limitation — Yes / No"
)
```

### Injury Past Year

```{r display-injury-past-year}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_needs_freq[["injury_past_year"]],
  col.names = c("Injury in past 12 months", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "injury_past_year — Yes / No"
)
```
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 6. Survey Design Variables

These variables are required for complex-sample variance estimation and cycle identification. Bootstrap weights (`bsw001`–`bsw500`) and the cluster PSU identifier are absent from the PUMF and require Statistics Canada's supplemental bootstrap file or RDC access.

```{r section-survey-design}
```

## 6.1 Survey Weights

```{r display-weights}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_weights_summary,
  col.names = c("Variable", "N valid", "N missing", "Mean", "SD",
                "Min", "P25", "Median", "P75", "Max"),
  caption   = "Table 6.1 — Survey weights: wts_m_pooled (halved for pooling) and wts_m_original",
  align     = c("l", rep("r", 9))
)
```

## 6.2 Survey Cycle

```{r display-cycle}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_cycle_freq,
  col.names = c("Survey cycle", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "Table 6.2 — cycle_f: CCHS survey cycle label"
)
```

## 6.3 Health Region Strata (geodpmf)

```{r display-geodpmf}
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  t_geodpmf_summary,
  caption = "Table 6.3 — geodpmf: health region proxy used as strata identifier",
  align   = c("l", "r", "r", "r", "l")
)
```

> **Missing bootstrap weights:** `bsw001`–`bsw500` are distributed by Statistics Canada in a separate supplemental file not bundled with the standard PUMF download. Without these, §5.3–5.10 bootstrap confidence intervals cannot be computed. **Missing cluster identifier:** PSU cluster codes are suppressed in the PUMF; full complex-sample variance estimation requires master file access.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Session Information

```{r session-info}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
cat("Report rendered:", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "\n\n")
cat("Elapsed time:", round(difftime(Sys.time(), report_render_start_time, units = "secs"), 1), "seconds\n\n")
sessionInfo()
```
